name: engineering-management
description: Skills for engineering managers and team leads — covering the tactical day-to-day mechanics (first-week snapshot, manager toolkit, 1-1s, feedback and reviews, hiring, motivation, stress and pressure, information and politics, self-care, team health, career growth) plus the longer-arc and cross-cutting work (project breakdown, hard conversations, the annual career vision exercise, and turning unspoken expectations into explicit agreements). Derived from James Stanier's Become an Effective Software Engineering Manager, Camille Fournier's The Manager's Path, and Niels Horeman's Leadership Essentials series.
